description: Fetch the high-resolution structure of streptavidin (PDB: 1STP) and its 2mFo-DFc electron density map (Hint: use PyMOL's `fetch 1stp, type=2fofc` command). Isolate the bound biotin ligand (residue name BTN). Create an isomesh object from the loaded map contoured at a 1.0 or 1.5 sigma level, and carve it exclusively around the BTN ligand with a ~1.5 to 2.0 Angstrom buffer. Color the mesh distinctly and display the ligand as sticks. Save the complete PyMOL session state to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/sessions/biotin_density.pse'. Render a high-resolution PNG figure and save it to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/images/biotin_mesh.png'. Write a text report to '/home/ga/PyMOL_Data/density_report.txt' containing: the PDB ID used, the ligand residue name, the sigma contour level you used, and the carving buffer distance in Angstroms.
